verses.text: 'Be ye followers of me, even as I also am of Christ'; he praises their keeping the ordinances. Follow the leader. Imitate him only as he follows Christ.
'Be ye followers of me, even as I also am of Christ'; he praises their keeping the ordinances. Follow the leader. Imitate him only as he follows Christ.
The head of the man is Christ, of the woman the man, of Christ is God. Order in headship. God has set a comely order in His house.
The head of the man is Christ, of the woman the man, of Christ is God. Order in headship. God has set a comely order in His house.
The head of the man is Christ, of the woman the man, of Christ is God. Order in headship. God has set a comely order in His house.
The head of the man is Christ, of the woman the man, of Christ is God. Order in headship. God has set a comely order in His house.
The man is the image of God, the woman the glory of the man. Honour and order. Each has a place in God's design.
The man is the image of God, the woman the glory of the man. Honour and order. Each has a place in God's design.
The man is the image of God, the woman the glory of the man. Honour and order. Each has a place in God's design.
The man is the image of God, the woman the glory of the man. Honour and order. Each has a place in God's design.
Yet 'neither is the man without the woman... in the Lord'; all things are of God. Mutual need. Neither is independent of the other or of God.
Yet 'neither is the man without the woman... in the Lord'; all things are of God. Mutual need. Neither is independent of the other or of God.
Judge in yourselves; nature itself teaches; we have no such custom of contention. Comely worship. Order and modesty befit the church.
Judge in yourselves; nature itself teaches; we have no such custom of contention. Comely worship. Order and modesty befit the church.
Judge in yourselves; nature itself teaches; we have no such custom of contention. Comely worship. Order and modesty befit the church.
Judge in yourselves; nature itself teaches; we have no such custom of contention. Comely worship. Order and modesty befit the church.
When ye come together it is 'not for the better,' for there are divisions among you. Sad gatherings. Strife mars their very worship.
When ye come together it is 'not for the better,' for there are divisions among you. Sad gatherings. Strife mars their very worship.
When ye come together it is 'not for the better,' for there are divisions among you. Sad gatherings. Strife mars their very worship.
Ye eat not the Lord's supper aright; one is hungry, another full; do ye despise the church? Disorder rebuked. Their feasting shamed the poor and the table.
Ye eat not the Lord's supper aright; one is hungry, another full; do ye despise the church? Disorder rebuked. Their feasting shamed the poor and the table.
Ye eat not the Lord's supper aright; one is hungry, another full; do ye despise the church? Disorder rebuked. Their feasting shamed the poor and the table.
The Lord took bread, 'this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.' The memorial. The supper recalls His broken body.
The Lord took bread, 'this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.' The memorial. The supper recalls His broken body.
This cup is 'the new testament in my blood'; ye 'shew the Lord's death till he come.' Proclaiming His death. The supper preaches the cross until He returns.
This cup is 'the new testament in my blood'; ye 'shew the Lord's death till he come.' Proclaiming His death. The supper preaches the cross until He returns.
Whoso eats unworthily is 'guilty of the body and blood'; 'let a man examine himself.' Self-examination. We must come to the table with a searched heart.
Whoso eats unworthily is 'guilty of the body and blood'; 'let a man examine himself.' Self-examination. We must come to the table with a searched heart.
Whoso eats unworthily is 'guilty of the body and blood'; 'let a man examine himself.' Self-examination. We must come to the table with a searched heart.
For this many are weak and sick; 'if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.' Chastened. God corrects His own to spare them final condemnation.
For this many are weak and sick; 'if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.' Chastened. God corrects His own to spare them final condemnation.
For this many are weak and sick; 'if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.' Chastened. God corrects His own to spare them final condemnation.
When ye come to eat, 'tarry one for another'; the rest I will order when I come. Wait in love. Reverent order is to mark the supper.
When ye come to eat, 'tarry one for another'; the rest I will order when I come. Wait in love. Reverent order is to mark the supper.
